title: Public frontend deployment blocked until the reconciled frontend framework-security P0 upgrade is accepted
affects: public frontend deployment / production exposure of apps/web
any production deploy or G7 release of the web app
detail: The current main frontend tree carries next==15.3.4 / react==19.1.0 / react-dom==19.1.0 / eslint-config-next==15.3.4, which are affected by React CVE-2025-55182 (CVSS 10.0 RSC RCE; 19.1.x fix = 19.1.2) and by the Next.js July-20-2026 security release (4 HIGH + 5 MEDIUM; Maintenance-LTS target 15.5.21). Until the reconciled frontend framework-security P0 upgrade task is ACCEPTED and its P0 security gates (advisory-free full lock, committed-lockfile age gate, blocking audit, npm-tooling advisory check) pass, the web frontend MUST NOT be publicly deployed or exposed. This is a deployment hold; it deliberately does NOT reference the upgrade task's own id in affects/detail so it cannot block that task's acceptance (accepting the task is what clears this hold). Note the API separately has no auth/RLS and is INTERNAL/DEV-only (B-001 chain).
exact_human_action: None to clear directly: this hold is cleared automatically when the orchestrator records acceptance of the reconciled frontend framework-security P0 upgrade (see gated_by_task) and confirms its security gates passed. Owner action is only to authorize that upgrade's dispatch and the age exception (B-013).
where_to_enter: Cleared by the orchestrator upon accepting the gating task; no owner data entry.
how_it_will_be_tested: A deploy/G7 checklist item asserts this blocker is resolved (gating task accepted + security gates green) before any public frontend deployment; CI/deploy definitions keep autoDeploy off until then.
